---
title: "Kreview Intelligence Dashboard"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme:
      - cyborg
      - custom.scss
    embed-resources: true
    html-math-method: katex
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        <style>
          .legend .scrollbox { clip-path: none !important; }
          g.legend rect.bg { fill-opacity: 0 !important; }
        </style>
jupyter: python3
execute:
  cache: false
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
#| echo: false
# Quarto injects -P params here; defaults are for local development only
matrix_path = "output/FSC_gene_matrix.parquet"
feature_name = "FSC_gene"
cvd_safe = False
shap_samples = 500
shap_features = 10
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
# ── Setup: imports, renderer config, data loading ──────────────────────────
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import json
import joblib
import warnings
from pathlib import Path
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import plotly.io as pio
from IPython.display import Markdown, HTML, display
import itables

from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

pio.renderers._render_on_display = False
_plotly_loaded = False

import os
def show_plotly(fig, filename=None):
    global _plotly_loaded
    if isinstance(fig, go.Figure):
        if filename:
            try:
                os.makedirs("static_plots", exist_ok=True)
                fig.write_image(f"static_plots/{filename}.png", scale=2)
            except Exception:
                pass
        fig.update_layout(margin=dict(b=40, t=30, l=10, r=10))
        html_str = fig.to_html(
            full_html=False,
            include_plotlyjs=True if not _plotly_loaded else False,
            include_mathjax=False,
            config={"responsive": True}
        )
        _plotly_loaded = True
        display(HTML(html_str))
    elif isinstance(fig, (str, Markdown)):
        display(fig)
    else:
        display(Markdown(f"> Unexpected output type: {type(fig)}"))

pio.templates.default = "plotly_dark"
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

from kreview.eval_engine import (
    LABEL_ORDER, LABEL_COLORS, plot_violin, plot_density,
    plot_feature_vs_vaf, plot_feature_importance, plot_roc_curves,
    set_theme
)

# Sanitize potential string injection from Quarto CLI
is_cvd_safe = str(cvd_safe).lower() == "true"

set_theme(is_cvd_safe)

# We will define THEME after loading model results to allow dynamic model colors.
LAYOUT_DEFAULTS = dict(
    autosize=True,
    paper_bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)',
    font=dict(color='#e0e6ed'),
    title_x=0.5,
    legend=dict(
        orientation="v",
        yanchor="top",
        y=-0.15,
        xanchor="center",
        x=0.5,
        font=dict(size=11, color="#c9d1d9")
    )
)

df = pd.read_parquet(matrix_path)

# Import canonical metadata columns — single source of truth, stays in sync
# with kreview.core automatically (includes split, n_ch_variants, etc.)
from kreview.core import LABEL_META_COLS
meta_cols = LABEL_META_COLS
numeric_cols = [c for c in df.select_dtypes(include=np.number).columns if c not in meta_cols]

eval_path = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_eval_stats.parquet"
if eval_path.exists():
    eval_df = pd.read_parquet(eval_path)
    # Prefer univariate_auc (model-aligned), fall back to cohens_d (legacy)
    if "univariate_auc" in eval_df.columns:
        best = eval_df.nlargest(1, "univariate_auc")
        top_col = best["feature_column"].iloc[0] if len(best) > 0 else numeric_cols[0]
    elif "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" in eval_df.columns:
        best = eval_df.dropna(subset=["cohens_d_true_vs_healthy"]).nlargest(1, "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy")
        top_col = best["feature_column"].iloc[0] if len(best) > 0 else numeric_cols[0]
    else:
        top_col = numeric_cols[0]
else:
    eval_df = None
    top_col = numeric_cols[0] if numeric_cols else None

# ── Load model results (CPU + GPU merge) ──
from kreview.eval_engine import load_model_results
_shap_display_n = int(shap_features)
mres = load_model_results(Path(matrix_path).parent, feature_name)
if mres is not None:
    top_feats = mres.get("top_features", numeric_cols)
    
    # Dynamic model discovery
    DYNAMIC_MODELS = []
    _name_map = {
        "lr": "Logistic Regression", "rf": "Random Forest", "xgb": "XGBoost",
        "tabpfn": "TabPFN", "tabpfn_ft": "TabPFN-FT",
        "tabicl": "TabICL", "tabicl_ft": "TabICL-FT",
    }
    for k in mres.keys():
        if k.startswith('auc_') and not k.endswith('_ci_lower') and not k.endswith('_ci_upper') and not k.startswith('auc_delta_'):
            m_id = k.replace('auc_', '')
            if m_id not in [x[0] for x in DYNAMIC_MODELS]:
                DYNAMIC_MODELS.append((m_id, _name_map.get(m_id, m_id.upper())))
else:
    mres = None
    auc_rf = auc_lr = auc_xgb = 0
    top_feats = numeric_cols
    DYNAMIC_MODELS = [("lr", "Logistic Regression"), ("rf", "Random Forest"), ("xgb", "XGBoost")]

# ── Color palette: vanilla/FT pairs share color families ──
if is_cvd_safe:
    _MODEL_COLORS = {
        "Logistic Regression": "#0072B2",  # Blue
        "Random Forest":       "#CC79A7",  # Rose
        "XGBoost":             "#F0E442",  # Yellow
        "TabPFN":              "#56B4E9",  # Sky Blue
        "TabPFN-FT":           "#009E73",  # Teal
        "TabICL":              "#E69F00",  # Gold
        "TabICL-FT":           "#D55E00",  # Vermillion
    }
else:
    _MODEL_COLORS = {
        "Logistic Regression": "#ff9b42",  # Orange
        "Random Forest":       "#E85D75",  # Hot Pink
        "XGBoost":             "#9C27B0",  # Purple
        "TabPFN":              "#42A5F5",  # Light Blue
        "TabPFN-FT":           "#1565C0",  # Dark Blue (FT = deeper shade)
        "TabICL":              "#66BB6A",  # Light Green
        "TabICL-FT":           "#2E7D32",  # Dark Green (FT = deeper shade)
    }

p_models = [_MODEL_COLORS.get(display, "#888") for _, display in DYNAMIC_MODELS]

THEME = {
    "labels": LABEL_COLORS,
    "models": p_models,
    "model_map": {display: p_models[i % len(p_models)] for i, (_, display) in enumerate(DYNAMIC_MODELS)},
    "bg": "#1a1d23",
    "grid": "#2e3440",
    "text": "#e0e6ed",
    "axis": "#88c0d0"
}


# Load Models
# Clamp SHAP display count to actual selected feature count (top_percentile may select fewer)
_shap_display_n = min(_shap_display_n, len(top_feats))
shap_df_sample = None
X_shap = None
y_shap = None
legacy_shap_preds = {}

best_preds_all = None
kde_labels = None
oof_y = None
oof_preds = {}

if mres is not None and top_feats:
    # ── Find Best Model Prefix ──
    all_aucs = {k.replace('auc_', ''): v for k, v in mres.items() if k.startswith('auc_') and not k.endswith(('_ci_lower', '_ci_upper', '_std'))}
    best_m = max(all_aucs, key=all_aucs.get) if all_aucs else None

    # ── D-01: Load OOF predictions for ROC & KDE (consistent with official AUC) ──
    _oof_lbl_key = next((k for k in mres if k == "oof_labels" or k.endswith("_oof_labels")), None)
    oof_y = np.array(mres[_oof_lbl_key]) if _oof_lbl_key else None
    
    for _pfx, _nice in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
        _key = f"{_pfx}_oof_probs"
        if _key in mres:
            oof_preds[_nice] = np.array(mres[_key])

    # ── S-03 fix: KDE density from OOF predictions (not retrained model) ──
    # Retrained model predictions on subsampled data leak training info.
    # OOF predictions are held-out and cover the full model cohort.
    _best_oof_key = f"{best_m}_oof_probs" if best_m else None
    if _best_oof_key and _best_oof_key in mres and _oof_lbl_key:
        best_preds_all = np.array(mres[_best_oof_key])
        # Map binary OOF labels back to string labels for KDE grouping
        model_mask = df["label"].isin([
            "True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+",
            "Healthy Normal", "Possible ctDNA-", "Possible ctDNA−"
        ])
        kde_labels = df.loc[model_mask, "label"].values
        if len(kde_labels) != len(best_preds_all):
            import structlog as _stlog; _stlog.get_logger().warning(
                "oof_length_mismatch", preds=len(best_preds_all), labels=len(kde_labels)
            )
            best_preds_all = None
            kde_labels = None
    elif best_m:
        # Legacy fallback for results generated before OOF saving
        best_joblib = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_{best_m}_model.joblib"
        if best_joblib.exists():
            try:
                model = joblib.load(best_joblib)
                if hasattr(model, "predict_proba"):
                    _kde_n = min(2000, len(df))
                    kde_idx = df.sample(n=_kde_n, random_state=99).index
                    best_preds_all = model.predict_proba(df.loc[kde_idx, top_feats].fillna(0))[:, 1]
                    kde_labels = df.loc[kde_idx, "label"].values
            except Exception as e:
                import structlog as _stlog; _stlog.get_logger().warning(f"{best_m}_kde_prediction_failed", error=str(e))

    # Filter to model-eligible labels only.
    # Undetermined samples (CH-only variants) are excluded by omission —
    # they are not in the inclusion list below, matching the exclusion
    # logic in build_binary_target() and _assign_train_test_split().
    model_label = df["label"].isin([
        "True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+",
        "Healthy Normal", "Possible ctDNA-", "Possible ctDNA−"
    ])

    # ── SHAP subsample (only used for SHAP explainability, NOT for ROC plots) ──
    shap_df = df[model_label]
    _max_shap = int(shap_samples)
    
    if len(shap_df) > 0:
        if len(shap_df) > _max_shap:
            sample_idx = shap_df.sample(n=_max_shap, random_state=42).index
        else:
            sample_idx = shap_df.index
        
        X_shap = shap_df.loc[sample_idx, top_feats].fillna(0)
        y_shap = shap_df.loc[sample_idx, "label"].isin(["True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+"]).astype(int).values
        shap_df_sample = shap_df.loc[sample_idx, ["label"] + (["sample_id"] if "sample_id" in shap_df.columns else [])].copy()

        # Generate legacy predictions for SHAP subsample ROC if OOF is not available
        if oof_y is None:
            for _pfx, _nice in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
                model_joblib = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_{_pfx}_model.joblib"
                if model_joblib.exists():
                    try:
                        model = joblib.load(model_joblib)
                        if hasattr(model, "predict_proba"):
                            legacy_shap_preds[_nice] = model.predict_proba(X_shap)[:, 1]
                    except Exception as e:
                        import structlog as _stlog; _stlog.get_logger().warning(f"{_pfx}_shap_prediction_failed", error=str(e))

    del shap_df
    import gc; gc.collect()

# ── Helper: format CI string ──
def _fmt_auc_ci(prefix):
    """Format AUC with 95% CI, e.g. '0.872 [0.84–0.91]'."""
    if mres is None:
        return "N/A"
    val = mres.get(f"auc_{prefix}", 0)
    lo = mres.get(f"auc_{prefix}_ci_lower")
    hi = mres.get(f"auc_{prefix}_ci_upper")
    if lo is not None and hi is not None:
        return f"{val:.3f} [{lo:.2f}–{hi:.2f}]"
    return f"{val:.3f}" if val else "N/A"

# ── Load scoreboard if available ──
scoreboard_df = None
scoreboard_path = Path(matrix_path).parent / "scoreboard_combined__all.parquet"
if scoreboard_path.exists():
    try:
        scoreboard_df = pd.read_parquet(scoreboard_path)
    except Exception as e:
        display(Markdown(f"> Scoreboard could not be loaded: {e}"))
```

# Glossary {.sidebar}

### ctDNA Label Definitions

**Positive Class**:

- **`True ctDNA+`**: Confirmed somatic variants in paired IMPACT tissue.
- **`Possible ctDNA+`**: Sub-threshold mutations consistent with circulating tumor traces.

**Negative Class**:

- **`Healthy Normal`**: Healthy volunteer plasma.
- **`Possible ctDNA−`**: Cancer patient with no detectable tumor DNA in plasma.

**Excluded from Training**:

- **`Undetermined`**: Somatic SNVs detected but all are clonal hematopoiesis hotspots with no SV/CNA evidence. Cannot be confidently classified.

---

**Train/Test Split**: Samples are deterministically assigned to train (80%) or test (20%) splits via stratified sampling with a fixed seed. Holdout AUC evaluates generalization on the test split only.

---

*Distinct fragmentation patterns from tumor-derived cfDNA may be detectable in plasma via size, motif, and nucleosome positioning signatures.*

[Methods & API Documentation](https://msk-access.github.io/kreview/)

# Executive Summary {orientation="rows" scrolling="true"}

## Row {height="15%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Cohort Size"
dict(value=f"{len(df):,}", color="primary", icon="people")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Positive Rate"
positive = sum(df["label"].isin(["True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+"]))
rate = positive / len(df) * 100 if len(df) > 0 else 0
dict(value=f"{rate:.1f}%", color="info", icon="percent")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Best AUC"
if mres is not None and DYNAMIC_MODELS:
    # find best model prefix by auc
    best_pfx = max(DYNAMIC_MODELS, key=lambda x: mres.get(f"auc_{x[0]}", 0))[0]
    best_str = _fmt_auc_ci(best_pfx)
else:
    best_str = "N/A"
dict(value=best_str, color="success", icon="trophy")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Sensitivity @ Optimal"
sens = "N/A"
if mres and best_m and f"{best_m}_classification_report" in mres:
    rep = mres[f"{best_m}_classification_report"]
    if "1" in rep:
        sens = f"{rep['1']['recall']:.2f}"
dict(value=sens, color="warning", icon="bullseye")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Specificity @ Optimal"
spec = "N/A"
if mres and best_m and f"{best_m}_classification_report" in mres:
    rep = mres[f"{best_m}_classification_report"]
    if "0" in rep:
        spec = f"{rep['0']['recall']:.2f}"
dict(value=spec, color="info", icon="shield-check")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Sens @ 100% Spec"
_s100 = "N/A"
if mres and best_m:
    # Dynamic: uses best_m (discovered from max AUC), not a hardcoded model
    _s100_val = mres.get(f"{best_m}_sensitivity_at_100spec")
    if _s100_val is not None:
        _n_det = mres.get(f"{best_m}_n_detected_at_100spec", "?")
        _s100 = f"{_s100_val:.2f} ({_n_det} det)"
dict(value=_s100, color="danger", icon="crosshair")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Holdout AUC"
_ho = "N/A"
_ho_color = "info"
if mres and best_m:
    _ho_val = mres.get(f"holdout_{best_m}_auc")
    if _ho_val is not None:
        _cv_auc = mres.get(f"auc_{best_m}", 0)
        _drop = _cv_auc - _ho_val if _cv_auc else None
        _drop_str = f" (\u0394{_drop:+.3f})" if _drop is not None else ""
        _ho = f"{_ho_val:.3f}{_drop_str}"
        # Dynamic color based on AUC drop severity
        if _drop is not None and _drop < 0.05:
            _ho_color = "success"
        elif _drop is not None and _drop < 0.10:
            _ho_color = "warning"
        else:
            _ho_color = "danger"
dict(value=_ho, color=_ho_color, icon="arrow-left-right")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Features Extracted"
dict(value=f"{len(numeric_cols)}", color="primary", icon="activity")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Verdict"
if mres is not None and DYNAMIC_MODELS:
    best = max(mres.get(f"auc_{pfx}", 0) for pfx, _ in DYNAMIC_MODELS)
    if best >= 0.80:
        verdict = "Strong separation"
        v_color = "success"
    elif best >= 0.70:
        verdict = "Moderate separation"
        v_color = "warning"
    else:
        verdict = "Weak separation"
        v_color = "danger"
else:
    verdict = "No model data"
    v_color = "secondary"
dict(value=verdict, color=v_color, icon="graph-up")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Nested CV"
_ncv = "No"
_ncv_color = "secondary"
if mres and mres.get("nested_cv"):
    _ncv = "Yes"
    _ncv_color = "info"
    # Show refit feature count for best model if available
    _refit = mres.get(f"{best_m}_refit_features") if best_m else None
    if _refit:
        _ncv = f"Yes ({len(_refit)} feats)"
dict(value=_ncv, color=_ncv_color, icon="layers")
```

## Row {height="40%"}

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "ROC Summary"
# D-01 fix: Use OOF predictions (same data as official AUC) instead of SHAP subsample.
# This ensures the ROC curve AUC matches the value box AUC exactly.
_roc_title = "Multi-Model ROC"
_roc_y = None
_roc_scores = {}

if oof_y is not None and len(oof_preds) > 0:
    # Primary path: OOF predictions from cross_val_predict
    _roc_y = oof_y
    _roc_scores = oof_preds
    _roc_title = "Multi-Model ROC (Out-of-Fold)"
elif y_shap is not None:
    # Legacy fallback: SHAP subsample predictions
    _roc_y = y_shap
    _roc_scores = legacy_shap_preds
    _roc_title = "Multi-Model ROC (SHAP Subsample \u2013 Legacy)"

if _roc_y is not None and len(_roc_scores) > 0:
    y_true_dict = {name: _roc_y for name in _roc_scores.keys()}
    fig_roc = plot_roc_curves(y_true_dict, _roc_scores, title=_roc_title)
    _ = fig_roc.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, height=350
    )
    colors = THEME["models"]
    for i, trace in enumerate(fig_roc.data):
        if trace.name != "Random":
            trace.line.color = colors[i % len(colors)]
    show_plotly(fig_roc, filename="roc_summary")
else:
    display(Markdown("> ROC data not available."))
```

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Calibration Reliability"
has_cal = False
if mres is not None:
    fig_cal = go.Figure()
    for i, (prefix, name) in enumerate(DYNAMIC_MODELS):
        cal = mres.get(f"{prefix}_calibration")
        if cal is not None:
            has_cal = True
            _ = fig_cal.add_trace(go.Scatter(
                x=cal["prob_pred"], y=cal["prob_true"],
                mode="lines+markers", name=name,
                line=dict(color=THEME["models"][i % len(THEME["models"])], width=2),
                marker=dict(size=8)
            ))
            
    if has_cal:
        _ = fig_cal.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=[0, 1], y=[0, 1],
            mode="lines", name="Perfectly Calibrated",
            line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash", width=1)
        ))
        _ = fig_cal.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
            height=350,
            title="Model Calibration",
            xaxis_title="Mean Predicted Probability",
            yaxis_title="Fraction of Positives"
        )
        show_plotly(fig_cal, filename="calibration")

if not has_cal:
    display(Markdown("> Calibration data not available."))
```

## Row {height="45%"}

### Column {width="60%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Evaluator Scoreboard"
if scoreboard_df is not None:
    display_cols = [
        "evaluator", "best_model", "best_auc",
        "holdout_auc", "auc_drop",
        "sensitivity", "specificity",
        "sens_at_100spec", "sens_at_95spec",
        "best_sens_100spec_healthy",
        "selection_method", "n_features", "best_subset_size",
        "nested_cv", "n_samples",
        "holdout_n_train", "holdout_n_test",
    ]
    avail = [c for c in display_cols if c in scoreboard_df.columns]
    itables.show(
        scoreboard_df[avail].round(3),
        classes="display compact nowrap",
        maxBytes=0,
        paging=True,
        pageLength=10
    )
else:
    display(Markdown("> Scoreboard not available. Run `kreview run` with multiple evaluators to generate."))
```

### Column {width="40%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Top 20 Features by Univariate AUC"
if eval_df is not None and "univariate_auc" in eval_df.columns:
    top_20_df = eval_df.sort_values(by="univariate_auc", ascending=False).head(20)
    top_20_df = top_20_df.iloc[::-1]

    fig_bar = px.bar(
        top_20_df, x="univariate_auc", y="feature_column",
        orientation='h',
        color="univariate_auc",
        color_continuous_scale="Purples"
    )
    _ = fig_bar.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
        showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Univariate AUC (CV)", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_bar, filename="top20_univariate_auc")
elif eval_df is not None and "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" in eval_df.columns:
    # Legacy fallback for pre-v0.0.9 eval stats
    top_20_df = eval_df.sort_values(by="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", ascending=False).head(20)
    top_20_df = top_20_df.iloc[::-1]
    fig_bar = px.bar(
        top_20_df, x="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", y="feature_column",
        orientation='h', color="cohens_d_true_vs_healthy",
        color_continuous_scale="Purples"
    )
    _ = fig_bar.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Cohen's D (legacy)", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_bar, filename="top20_effect_size")
```

# Model Validation {orientation="rows" scrolling="true"}

## Row {.tabset}

### Performance Metrics

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## Interpretation
Compares three classifier families on this feature set. Metrics are computed using out-of-fold predictions from stratified cross-validation.

- **Precision (PPV)**: Proportion of predicted positives that are truly positive.
- **Sensitivity (Recall)**: Proportion of actual positives correctly identified.
- **Specificity**: Proportion of actual negatives correctly identified.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
metric_records = []
if mres is not None:
    for prefix, nice_name in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
        report = mres.get(f"{prefix}_classification_report", {})
        if report and "1" in report:
            metric_records.append({"Model": nice_name, "Metric": "Precision (PPV)", "Score": report["1"]["precision"]})
            metric_records.append({"Model": nice_name, "Metric": "Sensitivity (Recall)", "Score": report["1"]["recall"]})
            metric_records.append({"Model": nice_name, "Metric": "F1-Score", "Score": report["1"]["f1-score"]})
            if "0" in report:
                metric_records.append({"Model": nice_name, "Metric": "Specificity (TNR)", "Score": report["0"]["recall"]})
            metric_records.append({"Model": nice_name, "Metric": "Accuracy", "Score": report.get("accuracy", 0)})

if metric_records:
    perf_df = pd.DataFrame(metric_records)
    fig_perf = px.bar(
        perf_df, x="Metric", y="Score", color="Model", barmode="group",
        color_discrete_sequence=THEME["models"]
    )
    _ = fig_perf.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1]),
        title="Cross-Validated Performance Metrics"
    )
    show_plotly(fig_perf, filename="performance_metrics")
else:
    display(Markdown("> Performance metrics not available."))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# AUC Deltas + Training Time summary
if mres is not None:
    summary_rows = []
    for prefix, name in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
        auc_val = mres.get(f"auc_{prefix}")
        t_sec = mres.get(f"{prefix}_training_time_sec")
        threshold = mres.get(f"{prefix}_optimal_threshold")
        if auc_val is not None:
            summary_rows.append({
                "Model": name,
                "AUC": f"{auc_val:.3f}",
                "Threshold (Youden J)": f"{threshold:.3f}" if threshold else "N/A",
                "Training Time (s)": f"{t_sec:.2f}" if t_sec else "N/A",
            })
            # Dynamic clinical metrics — auto-discover from mres keys
            _s100 = mres.get(f"{prefix}_sensitivity_at_100spec")
            if _s100 is not None:
                summary_rows[-1]["Sens@100%Spec"] = f"{_s100:.3f}"
            _s95 = mres.get(f"{prefix}_sensitivity_at_95spec")
            if _s95 is not None:
                summary_rows[-1]["Sens@95%Spec"] = f"{_s95:.3f}"
            _ho_auc = mres.get(f"holdout_{prefix}_auc")
            if _ho_auc is not None:
                summary_rows[-1]["Holdout AUC"] = f"{_ho_auc:.3f}"
    if summary_rows:
        # Dynamic AUC deltas: show pairwise differences from mres
        delta_parts = []
        for pfx, name in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
            # Check for any stored delta keys involving this model
            for k, v in mres.items():
                if k.startswith("auc_delta_") and pfx in k and isinstance(v, (int, float)):
                    label = k.replace("auc_delta_", "").upper().replace("_", "–")
                    if f"{label}: {v:+.3f}" not in delta_parts:
                        delta_parts.append(f"{label}: {v:+.3f}")
        if delta_parts:
            display(Markdown(f"**AUC Deltas**: {' | '.join(delta_parts)}"))
        itables.show(pd.DataFrame(summary_rows), classes="display compact nowrap", maxBytes=0, paging=False)
```

### ROC Curves with CI

```{python}
#| echo: false
# D-01 fix: Use OOF predictions for ROC with CI (same data as official AUC).
from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve as _roc_curve, roc_auc_score as _roc_auc

_ci_y = oof_y if oof_y is not None else y_shap
_ci_scores = oof_preds if len(oof_preds) > 0 else {}
if not _ci_scores and y_shap is not None:
    # Legacy fallback
    _ci_scores = legacy_shap_preds

if _ci_y is not None and len(_ci_scores) > 0:
    fig_roc2 = go.Figure()
    colors = THEME["models"]

    for i, (name, preds) in enumerate(_ci_scores.items()):
        fpr, tpr, _ = _roc_curve(_ci_y, preds)
        auc_val = _roc_auc(_ci_y, preds)
        
        # CI from mres (bootstrap CI computed during eval)
        # Build reverse map dynamically from DYNAMIC_MODELS (avoids KeyError for GPU models)
        _display_to_prefix = {display: pfx for pfx, display in DYNAMIC_MODELS}
        prefix = _display_to_prefix.get(name, name.lower())
        ci_lo = mres.get(f"auc_{prefix}_ci_lower") if mres else None
        ci_hi = mres.get(f"auc_{prefix}_ci_upper") if mres else None
        ci_str = f" [{ci_lo:.2f}\u2013{ci_hi:.2f}]" if ci_lo and ci_hi else ""
        
        _ = fig_roc2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=fpr, y=tpr, mode="lines",
            name=f"{name} (AUC={auc_val:.3f}{ci_str})",
            line=dict(color=colors[i % len(colors)], width=2)
        ))
    
    _ = fig_roc2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=[0, 1], y=[0, 1], mode="lines", name="Random",
        line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash", width=1)
    ))
    _ = fig_roc2.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, height=600,
        title="ROC Curves with 95% Bootstrap CI",
        xaxis_title="False Positive Rate", yaxis_title="True Positive Rate"
    )
    show_plotly(fig_roc2, filename="roc_with_ci")
else:
    display(Markdown("> ROC data not available."))
```

### Precision-Recall

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None:
    fig_pr = go.Figure()
    colors = THEME["models"]
    
    for i, (prefix, name) in enumerate(DYNAMIC_MODELS):
        pr = mres.get(f"{prefix}_pr_curve")
        ap = mres.get(f"{prefix}_avg_precision")
        if pr is not None and ap is not None:
            ap_str = f" (AP={ap:.3f})"
            _ = fig_pr.add_trace(go.Scatter(
                x=pr["recall"], y=pr["precision"],
                mode="lines", name=f"{name}{ap_str}",
                line=dict(color=colors[i % len(colors)], width=2)
            ))
    
    if len(fig_pr.data) > 0:
        # Add baseline (prevalence)
        prev = sum(df["label"].isin(["True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+"])) / len(df) if len(df) > 0 else 0.5
        _ = fig_pr.add_hline(y=prev, line_dash="dash", line_color="gray", opacity=0.5,
                             annotation_text=f"Prevalence ({prev:.2f})")
        _ = fig_pr.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, height=600,
            title="Precision-Recall Curves",
            xaxis_title="Recall", yaxis_title="Precision",
            xaxis=dict(range=[0, 1]), yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1])
        )
        show_plotly(fig_pr, filename="pr_curve")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> PR curve data not available. Re-run pipeline to generate."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Model results not loaded."))
```

### Decision Curve Analysis

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## Interpretation
Decision Curve Analysis shows the **net clinical benefit** of using the model vs treating all patients or treating none. A model is useful at thresholds where its curve is above both reference lines.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None:
    fig_dca = go.Figure()
    colors = THEME["models"]
    
    for i, (prefix, name) in enumerate(DYNAMIC_MODELS):
        dca = mres.get(f"{prefix}_dca")
        if dca is not None:
            _ = fig_dca.add_trace(go.Scatter(
                x=dca["thresholds"], y=dca["net_benefit_model"],
                mode="lines", name=name,
                line=dict(color=colors[i % len(colors)], width=2)
            ))
    
    # Add Treat All reference
    _dca_ref = mres.get(f"{best_m}_dca") if best_m else next((mres.get(f"{pfx}_dca") for pfx, _ in DYNAMIC_MODELS if f"{pfx}_dca" in mres), None)
    if _dca_ref:
        _ = fig_dca.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=_dca_ref["thresholds"], y=_dca_ref["net_benefit_treat_all"],
            mode="lines", name="Treat All",
            line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash", width=1)
        ))
    
    # Treat None is always 0
    _ = fig_dca.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dot", line_color="gray", opacity=0.5,
                          annotation_text="Treat None")
    
    if len(fig_dca.data) > 0:
        _ = fig_dca.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, height=600,
            title="Decision Curve Analysis",
            xaxis_title="Threshold Probability",
            yaxis_title="Net Benefit",
            xaxis=dict(range=[0, 1])
        )
        show_plotly(fig_dca, filename="dca")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> DCA data not available. Re-run pipeline to generate."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Model results not loaded."))
```

### Threshold Sensitivity

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None:
    _sweep_pfx = next((p for p, _ in DYNAMIC_MODELS if f"{p}_threshold_sweep" in mres), None)
    if _sweep_pfx:
        sweep = pd.DataFrame(mres[f"{_sweep_pfx}_threshold_sweep"])
        fig_sweep = go.Figure()
        
        for col, color, name in [
            ("sensitivity", THEME["labels"]["True ctDNA+"], "Sensitivity"),
            ("specificity", THEME["labels"]["Healthy Normal"], "Specificity"),
            ("ppv", THEME["models"][0], "PPV")
        ]:
            _ = fig_sweep.add_trace(go.Scatter(
                x=sweep["threshold"], y=sweep[col],
                mode="lines", name=name,
                line=dict(color=color, width=2)
            ))
        
        # Mark optimal threshold for this model
        opt = mres.get(f"{_sweep_pfx}_optimal_threshold")
        if opt is not None:
            _ = fig_sweep.add_vline(x=opt, line_dash="dash", line_color=THEME["axis"],
                                    annotation_text=f"Optimal ({opt:.3f})")
        
        _name = dict(DYNAMIC_MODELS).get(_sweep_pfx, _sweep_pfx.upper())
        _ = fig_sweep.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, height=600,
            title=f"{_name}: Sensitivity / Specificity / PPV vs Threshold",
            xaxis_title="Decision Threshold", yaxis_title="Score",
            xaxis=dict(range=[0, 1]), yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1])
        )
        show_plotly(fig_sweep, filename="threshold_sweep")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> Threshold sweep data not available. Re-run pipeline to generate."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Model results not loaded."))
```

### Fold Variability

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None:
    fold_data = []
    # Use OOF if available
    for prefix, name in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
        aucs = mres.get(f"{prefix}_fold_aucs")
        if aucs:
            for i, a in enumerate(aucs):
                fold_data.append({"Model": name, "Fold": i+1, "AUC": a})
    
    if fold_data:
        fold_df = pd.DataFrame(fold_data)
        fig_fold = px.box(
            fold_df, x="Model", y="AUC", color="Model",
            color_discrete_map=THEME["model_map"],
            points="all"
        )
        # GAP-7 fix: show per-model std instead of hardcoded RF-only
        _fold_stds = " | ".join(
            f"{m}: {mres.get(f'{p}_auc_std', 0):.3f}"
            for p, m in DYNAMIC_MODELS
            if f"{p}_fold_aucs" in mres
        )
        _fold_title = f"Cross-Validation Fold AUC Variability (std: {_fold_stds})" if _fold_stds else "Cross-Validation Fold AUC Variability"
        _ = fig_fold.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, height=400,
            title=_fold_title,
            showlegend=False,
            yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1])
        )
        show_plotly(fig_fold, filename="fold_variability")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> Fold-level data not available."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Fold AUC data not available. Re-run pipeline to generate."))
```

### Confusion Matrices

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## Interpretation
Confusion matrices evaluated at the Youden's J optimal threshold per model. All predictions are out-of-fold (unbiased).
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if oof_y is not None and len(oof_preds) > 0:
    models = DYNAMIC_MODELS
    titles = []
    cms = []
    
    for pfx, name in models:
        cm = mres.get(f"{pfx}_confusion_matrix")
        if cm:
            opt = mres.get(f"{pfx}_optimal_threshold")
            title_text = f"{name}<br><sub>(Cutoff: {opt:.3f})</sub>" if opt is not None else name
            titles.append(title_text)
            cms.append(np.array(cm))
            
    if cms:
        import io
        from contextlib import redirect_stdout
        
        with redirect_stdout(io.StringIO()):
            fig_cm = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=len(cms), subplot_titles=titles)
            labels = ["Negative (0)", "Positive (1)"]
            
            _cm_scales = ["Blues", "Reds", "Oranges", "Greens", "Purples", "Greys", "YlOrBr"]
            for i, cm in enumerate(cms):
                z_text = [[str(y) for y in x] for x in cm]
                trace = go.Heatmap(
                    z=cm, x=labels, y=labels,
                    text=z_text, texttemplate="%{text}",
                    colorscale=_cm_scales[i % len(_cm_scales)],
                    showscale=False
                )
                _ = fig_cm.add_trace(trace, row=1, col=i+1)
                
            _ = fig_cm.update_layout(
                **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
                title="Cross-Validated Confusion Matrices (Absolute Counts)",
                height=400
            )
            for i in range(len(cms)):
                _ = fig_cm.update_xaxes(title_text="Predicted Label", row=1, col=i+1)
                _ = fig_cm.update_yaxes(title_text="True Label", row=1, col=i+1, autorange="reversed")
            
        show_plotly(fig_cm)
    else:
        display(Markdown("> Confusion matrices not found."))
```

### Cancer Type Yield

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## Interpretation
Sensitivity stratified by cancer type using out-of-fold predictions. Identifies whether this feature generalizes across tumor origins or is tissue-specific.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None and "cancer_type_stats" in mres:
    ct_stats = mres["cancer_type_stats"]
    if ct_stats:
        ct_df = pd.DataFrame(ct_stats)
        
        # Discover all models that have _sensitivity in the dataframe
        val_vars = [col for col in ct_df.columns if col.endswith("_sensitivity")]
        
        if val_vars:
            melt_cols = ["cancer_type", "n_positives", "n_samples"]
            long_df = pd.melt(ct_df, id_vars=melt_cols, value_vars=val_vars, var_name="Model", value_name="Sensitivity")
            
            # Map prefix_sensitivity -> Display Name
            name_map = {f"{pfx}_sensitivity": name for pfx, name in DYNAMIC_MODELS}
            long_df["Model"] = long_df["Model"].map(lambda x: name_map.get(x, x.replace("_sensitivity", "").title()))
            long_df = long_df.dropna(subset=["Sensitivity"])
            
            if not long_df.empty:
                # Sort based on the best model's sensitivity (if available)
                best_sens = f"{best_m}_sensitivity" if best_m else val_vars[0]
                sort_col = best_sens if best_sens in ct_df.columns else val_vars[0]
                sort_order = ct_df.sort_values(by=sort_col, ascending=True)["cancer_type"].tolist()
                fig_ct = px.bar(
                    long_df, x="Sensitivity", y="cancer_type", color="Model", orientation="h",
                    barmode="group",
                    hover_data=["n_positives", "n_samples"],
                    category_orders={"cancer_type": sort_order},
                    color_discrete_map=THEME["model_map"],
                    title="Sensitivity by Cancer Subgroup"
                )
                _ = fig_ct.update_layout(
                    **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, xaxis_title="Sensitivity (Recall)",
                    yaxis_title="", height=600, legend_title=""
                )
                show_plotly(fig_ct, filename="sensitivity_by_cancer")
```

### Assay Yield

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## Interpretation
Sensitivity stratified by targeted sequencing assay version. Helps identify technical coverage bias.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
if mres is not None and "assay_stats" in mres:
    a_stats = mres["assay_stats"]
    if a_stats:
        a_df = pd.DataFrame(a_stats)
        
        val_vars = [col for col in a_df.columns if col.endswith("_sensitivity")]
        if val_vars:
            melt_cols = ["assay", "n_positives", "n_samples"]
            long_df = pd.melt(a_df, id_vars=melt_cols, value_vars=val_vars, var_name="Model", value_name="Sensitivity")
            
            name_map = {f"{pfx}_sensitivity": name for pfx, name in DYNAMIC_MODELS}
            long_df["Model"] = long_df["Model"].map(lambda x: name_map.get(x, x.replace("_sensitivity", "").title()))
            long_df = long_df.dropna(subset=["Sensitivity"])
            
            if not long_df.empty:
                best_sens = f"{best_m}_sensitivity" if best_m else val_vars[0]
                sort_col = best_sens if best_sens in a_df.columns else val_vars[0]
                sort_order = a_df.sort_values(by=sort_col, ascending=True)["assay"].tolist()
                fig_a = px.bar(
                    long_df, x="Sensitivity", y="assay", color="Model", orientation="h",
                    barmode="group",
                    hover_data=["n_positives", "n_samples"],
                    category_orders={"assay": sort_order},
                    color_discrete_map=THEME["model_map"],
                    title="Sensitivity by Assay"
                )
                _ = fig_a.update_layout(
                    **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, xaxis_title="Sensitivity (Recall)",
                    yaxis_title="", height=450, legend_title=""
                )
                show_plotly(fig_a)
```

### Classification Report

```{python}
#| echo: false
if oof_y is not None and len(oof_preds) > 0:
    rep_dfs = []
    from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, precision_recall_curve, average_precision_score
    for prefix, name in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
        if name in oof_preds:
            # R4 fix: use per-model Youden J optimal threshold (consistent with confusion matrices)
            _cls_thresh = mres.get(f"{prefix}_optimal_threshold", 0.5) if mres else 0.5
            d = classification_report(oof_y, (oof_preds[name] > _cls_thresh).astype(int), output_dict=True)
            t = pd.DataFrame(d).transpose()
            t["Model"] = name
            t.index.name = "Class/Metric"
            t = t.reset_index()
            rep_dfs.append(t)
            
    if rep_dfs:
        combined = pd.concat(rep_dfs, ignore_index=True)
        cols = ["Model", "Class/Metric", "precision", "recall", "f1-score", "support"]
        combined = combined[[c for c in cols if c in combined.columns]]
        combined.replace({"0": "ctDNA Negative", "1": "ctDNA Positive"}, inplace=True)
        itables.show(combined.round(3), classes="display nowrap", maxBytes=0)
```

### Feature Importances

```{python}
#| echo: false
has_imp = False
if mres is not None:
    # R11 fix: iterate models sorted by AUC descending to show the best model's importances
    _sorted_models = sorted(DYNAMIC_MODELS, key=lambda x: mres.get(f"auc_{x[0]}", 0), reverse=True)
    for pfx, name in _sorted_models:
        imp = mres.get(f"{pfx}_feature_importances")
        if imp and isinstance(imp, dict):
            has_imp = True
            imp_df = pd.DataFrame(
                sorted(imp.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:20],
                columns=["Feature", "Importance"]
            ).iloc[::-1]
            fig_imp = px.bar(
                imp_df, x="Importance", y="Feature", orientation="h",
                color="Importance", color_continuous_scale="Oranges"
            )
            _ = fig_imp.update_layout(
                **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
                title=f"{name} Feature Importances (Top 20)",
                margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10),
                xaxis_title="Importance", yaxis_title=""
            )
            show_plotly(fig_imp, filename=f"feature_importances_{pfx}")
            break  # Show only the best model that supports feature importances

if not has_imp:
    display(Markdown("> Feature importances not available."))
```

# Feature Explanation (SHAP) {orientation="rows" scrolling="false"}

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" icon="false"}
## About SHAP Explainability
SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) values explain model behavior by indicating which features most influenced each prediction. Higher absolute SHAP values indicate stronger influence. SHAP is model-relative — it explains *how the model decides*, not biological causality.
:::

## Row {.tabset}

```{python}
#| echo: false
# SHAP Helper functions
# R17 fix: import shap with graceful fallback
try:
    import shap
    _shap_available = True
except ImportError:
    _shap_available = False
    display(Markdown("> ⚠ `shap` package is not installed. SHAP explainability tabs will be skipped."))

def compute_shap_and_plot_beeswarm(model, X_sh, shap_df_samp, model_title="", n_display=10):
    """Compute SHAP values and render an interactive beeswarm plot.

    Args:
        model: Fitted tree-based model (RF or XGBoost).
        X_sh: Feature DataFrame for SHAP computation.
        shap_df_samp: DataFrame with 'label' and optional 'sample_id' columns.
        model_title: Title prefix for the plot.
        n_display: Number of top features to display.

    Returns:
        Tuple of (shap.Explanation, top_feature_indices, sample_labels, sample_ids).
        All None if computation fails.
    """
    if model is None or shap_df_samp is None:
        return None, None, None, None
    try:    
        exp = shap.TreeExplainer(model)
        sh_vals = exp.shap_values(X_sh, check_additivity=False)

        if isinstance(sh_vals, list):
            sv_values = sh_vals[1]
        elif getattr(sh_vals, "ndim", 0) == 3:
            sv_values = sh_vals[:, :, 1]
        else:
            sv_values = sh_vals

        base_val = exp.expected_value
        if isinstance(base_val, (list, np.ndarray)):
            base_val = base_val[1] if len(base_val) > 1 else base_val[0]

        sv = shap.Explanation(
            values=sv_values,
            base_values=base_val,
            data=X_sh.values,
            feature_names=X_sh.columns.tolist(),
        )
        
        feature_names = X_sh.columns.tolist()
        vals = np.abs(sv.values).mean(axis=0)
        top_idx = np.argsort(vals)[-n_display:]
        top_features = [feature_names[i] for i in top_idx]
        
        sample_labels = shap_df_samp["label"].values
        s_ids = shap_df_samp["sample_id"].values if "sample_id" in shap_df_samp.columns else shap_df_samp.index.values
        
        records = []
        for rank, i in enumerate(top_idx):
            feature = feature_names[i]
            for j in range(len(sv.values)):
                records.append({
                    'Feature': feature,
                    'Feature_Idx': rank,
                    'SHAP Impact': float(sv.values[j, i]),
                    'Biomarker Value': float(sv.data[j, i]) if sv.data is not None else 0.0,
                    'True Label': sample_labels[j],
                    'Sample ID': s_ids[j]
                })
        
        df_sv = pd.DataFrame(records)
        df_sv['Feature Value'] = df_sv.groupby('Feature')['Biomarker Value'].transform(lambda x: (x - x.min()) / (x.max() - x.min() + 1e-8))
        df_sv['Y'] = df_sv['Feature_Idx'] + np.random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25, size=len(df_sv))
        
        fig = px.scatter(df_sv, x='SHAP Impact', y='Y', color='Feature Value', 
                       hover_data=['Sample ID', 'True Label', 'Feature', 'Biomarker Value'],
                       color_continuous_scale=[[0, '#1E88E5'], [0.5, '#9C27B0'], [1, '#E53935']])
        fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=4, opacity=0.8, line=dict(width=0)))
        fig.update_layout(
            title=f"<b>{model_title}</b>: SHAP Feature Impact", title_x=0.5,
            plot_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], paper_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], font_color=THEME['text'], height=500,
            xaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'], zerolinecolor=THEME['axis'], zerolinewidth=2),
            yaxis=dict(showgrid=False, title='', tickmode='array', tickvals=list(range(len(top_features))), ticktext=top_features),
            coloraxis_colorbar=dict(title=""), margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=20)
        )
        show_plotly(fig)
        return sv, top_idx, sample_labels, s_ids
    except Exception as e:
        display(Markdown(f"> Error computing SHAP for {model_title}: {e}"))
        return None, None, None, None

def plot_shap_dependence(sv, top_idx, feature_names, sample_labels, s_ids, model_title=""):
    """Plot SHAP dependence for the top-2 features, colored by interaction.

    Args:
        sv: shap.Explanation object from compute_shap_and_plot_beeswarm.
        top_idx: Array of top feature indices (sorted by mean |SHAP|).
        feature_names: List of all feature names.
        sample_labels: Array of string labels for each sample.
        s_ids: Array of sample IDs for hover info.
        model_title: Title prefix for the plot.
    """
    if sv is None: return
    feat1 = feature_names[top_idx[-1]]
    top_2_idx = top_idx[-2] if len(top_idx) > 1 else top_idx[-1]
    feat2 = feature_names[top_2_idx]
    
    df_dep = pd.DataFrame({
        feat1: sv.data[:, top_idx[-1]], 'SHAP Impact': sv.values[:, top_idx[-1]],
        feat2: sv.data[:, top_2_idx], 'True Label': sample_labels, 'Sample ID': s_ids
    })
    fig = px.scatter(df_dep, x=feat1, y='SHAP Impact', color=feat2, hover_data=['Sample ID', 'True Label'],
                     color_continuous_scale=[[0, '#1E88E5'], [0.5, '#9C27B0'], [1, '#E53935']])
    fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=5, opacity=0.8, line=dict(width=0)))
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"<b>{model_title}</b><br>Interaction: {feat1} vs {feat2}", title_x=0.5,
        plot_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], paper_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], font_color=THEME['text'], height=450,
        xaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'], zerolinecolor=THEME['axis'], zerolinewidth=2),
        yaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'], zerolinecolor=THEME['axis'], zerolinewidth=2),
        margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=50, b=20)
    )
    show_plotly(fig)

def plot_shap_waterfall(sv, probs, feature_names, sample_labels, model_title=""):
    """Plot SHAP waterfall traces for the best TP and worst FP predictions.

    Args:
        sv: shap.Explanation object.
        probs: Predicted probability array (must match sample_labels length).
        feature_names: List of feature names.
        sample_labels: Array of string labels for each sample.
        model_title: Title prefix for the plot.
    """
    if sv is None or probs is None:
        return
    if len(probs) != len(sample_labels):
        display(Markdown(f"> SHAP waterfall skipped for {model_title}: probs/labels length mismatch ({len(probs)} vs {len(sample_labels)})"))
        return
    df_tracker = pd.DataFrame({'True Label': sample_labels, 'Probability': probs})
    
    tp_mask = df_tracker['True Label'] == "True ctDNA+"
    best_tp_idx = df_tracker[tp_mask]['Probability'].idxmax() if tp_mask.sum() > 0 else 0
    
    fp_mask = df_tracker['True Label'] == "Healthy Normal"
    worst_fp_idx = df_tracker[fp_mask]['Probability'].idxmax() if fp_mask.sum() > 0 else 0
    
    base_val = sv.base_values[0] if isinstance(sv.base_values, np.ndarray) and sv.base_values.size>0 else (sv.base_values if np.isscalar(sv.base_values) else 0)
    
    fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=(
        f"<b>True Positive</b> (Prob: {probs[best_tp_idx]:.2f})",
        f"<b>False Positive</b> (Prob: {probs[worst_fp_idx]:.2f})"
    ))
    
    for row_idx, s_idx in enumerate([best_tp_idx, worst_fp_idx]):
        shap_vals = sv.values[s_idx]
        order = np.argsort(np.abs(shap_vals))[-10:] 
        x = ["Baseline"] + [feature_names[i] for i in order] + ["Final"]
        y = [base_val] + [shap_vals[i] for i in order] + [base_val + np.sum(shap_vals)]
        
        hover_text = [f"Baseline: {base_val:.2f}"]
        for i in order:
            feat_val = sv.data[s_idx, i] if sv.data is not None else 0
            hover_text.append(f"Val: {feat_val:.3f}<br>Push: {shap_vals[i]:+.3f}")
        hover_text.append(f"Final: {y[-1]:.2f}")
        
        measure = ["absolute"] + ["relative"] * len(order) + ["total"]
        waterfall_trace = go.Waterfall(
            orientation="v", measure=measure, x=x, y=y, textposition="none", hovertext=hover_text, hoverinfo="x+text",
            decreasing={"marker":{"color":"#1E88E5"}}, increasing={"marker":{"color":"#E53935"}}, totals={"marker":{"color":"#9C27B0"}}
        )
        fig.add_trace(waterfall_trace, row=1, col=row_idx+1)
        
    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"<b>{model_title}</b>: Prediction Traces (TP vs FP)", title_x=0.5,
        plot_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], paper_bgcolor=THEME['bg'], font_color=THEME['text'], height=450, showlegend=False, margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=60, b=20)
    )
    fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=False, tickangle=-45)
    fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor=THEME['grid'])
    show_plotly(fig)
```

### SHAP Beeswarm

```{python}
#| echo: false
shap_results = {}
for prefix, display_name in DYNAMIC_MODELS:
    model_joblib = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_{prefix}_model.joblib"
    if model_joblib.exists():
        try:
            model = joblib.load(model_joblib)
            sv, top, sl, sid = compute_shap_and_plot_beeswarm(model, X_shap, shap_df_sample, display_name, n_display=_shap_display_n)
            if sv is not None:
                shap_results[prefix] = (sv, top, sl, sid, display_name)
        except Exception as e:
            import structlog as _stlog; _stlog.get_logger().warning(f"{prefix}_shap_beeswarm_failed", error=str(e))
    elif mres and f"{prefix}_shap" in mres:
        # Fallback: pre-computed SHAP values from JSON (GPU models with --skip-gpu-joblib)
        try:
            _shap_data = mres[f"{prefix}_shap"]
            _sv_arr = np.array(_shap_data["shap_values"])
            _feat_names = _shap_data.get("feature_names", [])
            
            # Render a mean |SHAP| bar chart (no beeswarm without fitted model)
            mean_abs = np.abs(_sv_arr).mean(axis=0)
            top_idx = np.argsort(mean_abs)[::-1][:_shap_display_n]
            top_names = [_feat_names[i] if i < len(_feat_names) else f"f{i}" for i in top_idx]
            top_vals = [float(mean_abs[i]) for i in top_idx]
            
            fig_shap_bar = go.Figure(go.Bar(
                x=top_vals[::-1], y=top_names[::-1],
                orientation="h",
                marker_color=THEME["models"][DYNAMIC_MODELS.index((prefix, display_name)) % len(THEME["models"])]
            ))
            _ = fig_shap_bar.update_layout(
                **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, height=max(300, 30 * len(top_names)),
                title=f"{display_name} — Mean |SHAP| (Pre-computed, no joblib)",
                xaxis_title="Mean |SHAP value|"
            )
            show_plotly(fig_shap_bar, filename=f"shap_bar_{prefix}")
        except Exception as e:
            import structlog as _stlog; _stlog.get_logger().warning(f"{prefix}_precomputed_shap_failed", error=str(e))
```

### Feature Dependence

```{python}
#| echo: false
for prefix, (sv, top, sl, sid, display_name) in shap_results.items():
    try:
        plot_shap_dependence(sv, top, X_shap.columns.tolist(), sl, sid, display_name)
    except Exception as e:
        display(Markdown(f"> SHAP dependence plot skipped for {display_name}: {e}"))
```

### Prediction Traces (Waterfalls)

```{python}
#| echo: false
for prefix, (sv, top, sl, sid, display_name) in shap_results.items():
    try:
        # R3 fix: oof_preds is keyed by display name (e.g., "Random Forest"), not prefix ("rf")
        preds = oof_preds.get(display_name)
        if preds is None:
            model_joblib = Path(matrix_path).parent / f"{feature_name}_{prefix}_model.joblib"
            if model_joblib.exists():
                model = joblib.load(model_joblib)
                if hasattr(model, "predict_proba"):
                    preds = model.predict_proba(X_shap)[:, 1]
                else:
                    preds = model.predict(X_shap)
        plot_shap_waterfall(sv, preds, X_shap.columns.tolist(), sl, display_name)
    except Exception as e:
        display(Markdown(f"> SHAP waterfall skipped for {display_name}: {e}"))
```

### Feature Cards

```{python}
#| echo: false
try:
    from kreview.feature_cards import build_feature_cards, get_card_for_feature
    cards = build_feature_cards()
    
    # Get top features and build display cards
    display_feats = top_feats[:5] if len(top_feats) >= 5 else top_feats
    
    card_html = '<div style="display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 12px;">'
    for feat in display_feats:
        card = get_card_for_feature(feat, cards)
        
        # Get stats from eval_df
        d_val = ""
        miss_val = ""
        if eval_df is not None and "feature_column" in eval_df.columns:
            row = eval_df[eval_df["feature_column"] == feat]
            if len(row) > 0:
                auc_val = row["univariate_auc"].iloc[0] if "univariate_auc" in row.columns else None
                mi_val = row["mutual_info"].iloc[0] if "mutual_info" in row.columns else None
                parts = []
                if pd.notna(auc_val):
                    parts.append(f"AUC: {auc_val:.3f}")
                if pd.notna(mi_val):
                    parts.append(f"MI: {mi_val:.3f}")
                if not parts:
                    # Legacy fallback
                    cd = row["cohens_d_true_vs_healthy"].iloc[0] if "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" in row.columns else None
                    if pd.notna(cd):
                        parts.append(f"Cohen's d: {cd:.2f}")
                d_val = " | ".join(parts)
                if "pct_missing" in row.columns:
                    miss_val = f"Missing: {row['pct_missing'].iloc[0]:.1f}%"
        
        # Get feature stability
        stab = ""
        if mres and "feature_stability" in mres:
            s = mres["feature_stability"].get(feat)
            if s is not None:
                stab = f"Stability: {s:.0%}"
        
        # R13 fix: per-feature LR coefficient direction (not global)
        direction = ""
        if mres and "lr_feature_directions" in mres:
            _dir_val = mres["lr_feature_directions"].get(feat)
            if _dir_val:
                direction = f"LR: {_dir_val}"
        
        # Build metadata line — only show separator if both parts present
        _meta_parts = [p for p in [stab, direction] if p]
        _meta_line = " · ".join(_meta_parts)
        
        if card:
            card_html += f'''
            <div style="background: #1a1d23; border: 1px solid rgba(255,155,66,0.2); border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; width: 280px;">
                <div style="font-size: 0.75rem; color: #88c0d0; margin-bottom: 4px;">🧬 {card["tier_label"]} · {card["category"]}</div>
                <div style="font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 600; color: #e0e6ed; margin-bottom: 6px;">{feat}</div>
                <div style="font-size: 0.78rem; color: #8b949e;">Source: {card["source_file"]}</div>
                <div style="font-size: 0.78rem; color: #8b949e;">{d_val} {(" · " + miss_val) if miss_val else ""}</div>
                {f'<div style="font-size: 0.78rem; color: #8b949e;">{_meta_line}</div>' if _meta_line else ""}
                {"<div style='font-size: 0.7rem; color: #ff9b42; margin-top: 4px;'>Derived: " + ", ".join(card["derived_types"]) + "</div>" if card["has_derived"] else ""}
            </div>'''
        else:
            card_html += f'''
            <div style="background: #1a1d23; border: 1px solid rgba(255,155,66,0.1); border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; width: 280px;">
                <div style="font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 600; color: #e0e6ed;">{feat}</div>
                <div style="font-size: 0.78rem; color: #8b949e;">{d_val}</div>
            </div>'''
    
    card_html += '</div>'
    display(HTML(card_html))
except Exception as e:
    display(Markdown(f"> Feature cards could not be generated: {e}"))
```

### Probability Densities

```{python}
#| echo: false
if best_preds_all is not None and kde_labels is not None:
    from plotly.figure_factory import create_distplot
    
    probs = best_preds_all
    hist_data = []
    group_labels = []
    colors = []
    
    for lbl in LABEL_ORDER:
        mask = kde_labels == lbl
        d = probs[mask]
        if len(d) > 2:
            hist_data.append(d)
            group_labels.append(lbl)
            colors.append(THEME["labels"].get(lbl, "gray"))
            
    if len(hist_data) > 0:
        fig_kde = create_distplot(hist_data, group_labels, colors=colors,
                                  show_hist=False, show_rug=False)
        _ = fig_kde.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
            title=f"{dict(DYNAMIC_MODELS).get(best_m, best_m.upper() if best_m else 'Best Model')} Predicted Probability Density by Label",
            xaxis_title="Predicted Probability (ctDNA+)",
            yaxis_title="Density",
            height=600
        )
        show_plotly(fig_kde, filename="kde_distribution")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> Not enough samples per class for density plotting."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Prediction density data not available."))
```

# Biomarker Yield {orientation="columns" scrolling="true"}

## Column {width="60%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Volcano Plot: Predictive Power vs Significance"
_has_auc = eval_df is not None and "univariate_auc" in eval_df.columns
_has_cd = eval_df is not None and "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" in eval_df.columns
_x_col = "univariate_auc" if _has_auc else ("cohens_d_true_vs_healthy" if _has_cd else None)

if _x_col and eval_df is not None:
    eval_volcano = eval_df.copy()
    if "kw_pvalue" in eval_volcano.columns:
        eval_volcano["-log10(p)"] = -np.log10(eval_volcano["kw_pvalue"].replace(0, 1e-300))
        eval_volcano["is_top"] = eval_volcano["feature_column"] == top_col

        # NaN-safe hover text
        def _volcano_hover(row):
            auc = row.get('univariate_auc')
            cd = row.get('cohens_d_true_vs_healthy')
            kw = row.get('kw_pvalue')
            mi = row.get('mutual_info')
            parts = [f"<b>{row['feature_column']}</b>"]
            if pd.notna(auc): parts.append(f"AUC: {auc:.3f}")
            if pd.notna(mi): parts.append(f"MI: {mi:.3f}")
            if pd.notna(cd): parts.append(f"Cohen's D: {cd:.2f}")
            if pd.notna(kw): parts.append(f"K-W p: {kw:.3e}")
            return "<br>".join(parts)

        eval_volcano["hover_text"] = eval_volcano.apply(_volcano_hover, axis=1)

        fig_vol = px.scatter(
            eval_volcano, x=_x_col, y="-log10(p)",
            hover_name="hover_text", color="is_top",
            color_discrete_map={True: THEME["labels"]["True ctDNA+"], False: "#515865"}
        )
        _ = fig_vol.add_hline(y=-np.log10(0.01), line_dash="dash", line_color="#8DDA77", opacity=0.5)
        if _x_col == "univariate_auc":
            _ = fig_vol.add_vline(x=0.6, line_dash="dash", line_color="#8DDA77", opacity=0.5,
                                  annotation_text="AUC=0.6")
        else:
            _ = fig_vol.add_vline(x=0.8, line_dash="dash", line_color="#8DDA77", opacity=0.5)

        _xlabel = "Univariate AUC (larger = better discrimination)" if _has_auc else "Cohen's D (legacy)"
        _ = fig_vol.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
            margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=30, b=10),
            xaxis_title=_xlabel,
            yaxis_title="-Log10(Kruskal-Wallis P-Value)"
        )
        show_plotly(fig_vol, filename="volcano")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> Kruskal-Wallis p-values not available."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Evaluation statistics not found."))
```

```{python}
#| title: "Feature Selection: AUC vs Mutual Information"
# Show how features were selected (mRMR or hybrid union)
if eval_df is not None and "univariate_auc" in eval_df.columns and "mutual_info" in eval_df.columns:
    _sel_df = eval_df.copy()
    _top_set = set(mres.get("top_features", [])) if mres else set()
    _sqc = mres.get("selection_qc", {}) if mres else {}
    _method = _sqc.get("method", "unknown")
    _n_keep = _sqc.get("n_keep_per_metric", 0)

    if _method == "mrmr" and _top_set:
        # mRMR: binary Selected/Dropped — no AUC/MI overlap logic applies
        _sel_df["selection"] = _sel_df["feature_column"].apply(
            lambda f: "Selected (mRMR)" if f in _top_set else "Dropped"
        )
    elif _top_set and _n_keep > 0:
        _top_auc = set(_sel_df.nlargest(_n_keep, "univariate_auc")["feature_column"])
        _top_mi = set(_sel_df.nlargest(_n_keep, "mutual_info")["feature_column"])

        def _sel_category(feat):
            in_auc = feat in _top_auc
            in_mi = feat in _top_mi
            if in_auc and in_mi: return "Selected (Both)"
            if in_auc: return "Selected (AUC Only)"
            if in_mi: return "Selected (MI Only)"
            return "Dropped"

        _sel_df["selection"] = _sel_df["feature_column"].apply(_sel_category)
    elif _top_set:
        _sel_df["selection"] = _sel_df["feature_column"].apply(
            lambda f: "Selected" if f in _top_set else "Dropped"
        )
    else:
        _sel_df["selection"] = "Unknown"

    fig_sel = px.scatter(
        _sel_df, x="univariate_auc", y="mutual_info",
        color="selection",
        color_discrete_map={
            "Selected (mRMR)": "#00E5FF",      # Cyan
            "Selected (Both)": "#8DDA77",      # Green
            "Selected (AUC Only)": "#4FC3F7",  # Blue
            "Selected (MI Only)": "#FF9B42",   # Orange
            "Dropped": "#515865",              # Gray
            "Selected": "#8DDA77",
            "Unknown": "#999999",
        },
        hover_name="feature_column",
        opacity=0.7,
        category_orders={"selection": ["Selected (mRMR)", "Selected (Both)", "Selected (AUC Only)", "Selected (MI Only)", "Dropped"]}
    )
    _ = fig_sel.update_layout(
        **{**LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, "legend": dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.25, xanchor="center", x=0.5)},
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=30, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Univariate AUC (linear predictive power)",
        yaxis_title="Mutual Information (non-linear relevance)",
    )
    show_plotly(fig_sel, filename="selection_scatter")

    # mRMR disclaimer: AUC/MI axes are observational, not the selection criteria
    if _method == "mrmr":
        display(Markdown(
            "> **Note:** Features were selected using mRMR (F-statistic relevance + "
            "Pearson redundancy). They are projected onto Univariate AUC and Mutual "
            "Information for observational context only. A feature with lower AUC/MI "
            "may be selected if it provides highly unique, non-redundant signal."
        ))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Feature selection scatter requires univariate AUC and mutual information scores."))
```

if top_col:
    fig_v = px.violin(
        df, x="label", y=top_col, color="label",
        box=True, points="outliers",
        category_orders={"label": LABEL_ORDER},

        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_v, filename="top_feature_violin")

    if len(cancer) > 0:
        fig_vaf = px.scatter(
            cancer, x="max_vaf", y=top_col, color="label",

            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
            margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10)

        show_plotly(fig_vaf, filename="vaf_scatter")

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Statistical Ledger
```{python}
#| echo: false
if eval_df is not None:
    display_cols = ["feature_column", "univariate_auc", "mutual_info",
                    "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", "kw_pvalue",
                    "mwu_True_ctDNA_pos_vs_Healthy_Normal_effect_size", "spearman_vaf_r",
                    "pct_missing", "is_zero_variance"]
    avail = [c for c in display_cols if c in eval_df.columns]
    # Sort by best available model-aligned metric
    _sort_col = next(
        (c for c in ["univariate_auc", "mutual_info", "cohens_d_true_vs_healthy", "kw_statistic"]
         if c in eval_df.columns),
        None
    )
    if _sort_col:
        eval_sorted = eval_df.sort_values(by=_sort_col, ascending=False)
    else:
        eval_sorted = eval_df  # no sortable column, show as-is
    itables.show(eval_sorted[avail].round(4), classes="display nowrap dataTable", maxBytes=0)
```
:::

positive = sum(df["label"].isin(["True ctDNA+", "Possible ctDNA+"]))
dict(value=f"{positive:,} ({positive/len(df)*100:.1f}%)" if len(df) > 0 else "0", color="warning", icon="heart-pulse")

zv = 0
if eval_df is not None and "is_zero_variance" in eval_df.columns:
    zv = int(eval_df["is_zero_variance"].sum())
dict(value=f"{zv}", color="danger" if zv > 0 else "success", icon="exclamation-triangle" if zv > 0 else "check-circle")

## Row {height="40%"}

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Class Balance by Label"
label_counts = df["label"].value_counts()
fig_bal = px.bar(
    x=label_counts.index, y=label_counts.values,
    color=label_counts.index,
    color_discrete_map=THEME["labels"],
    text=[f"{v} ({v/len(df)*100:.1f}%)" if len(df) > 0 else f"{v}" for v in label_counts.values]
)
_ = fig_bal.update_layout(
    **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
    xaxis_title="", yaxis_title="Samples"
)
_ = fig_bal.update_traces(textposition="outside")
show_plotly(fig_bal, filename="class_balance")
```

```{python}
#| title: "Label × Cancer Type"
if "CANCER_TYPE" in df.columns:
    sunburst_df = df.groupby(["label", "CANCER_TYPE"]).size().reset_index(name="count")
    if len(sunburst_df) > 0:
        fig_sun = px.sunburst(
            sunburst_df, path=["label", "CANCER_TYPE"], values="count",
            color="label", color_discrete_map=THEME["labels"]
        )
        _ = fig_sun.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS,
            margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10)
        )
        show_plotly(fig_sun, filename="label_cancer_sunburst")
else:
    display(Markdown("> CANCER_TYPE column not available."))
```

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Cancer Type Distribution"
if "CANCER_TYPE" in df.columns:
    c_counts = df["CANCER_TYPE"].value_counts().nlargest(15)
    fig_dc = px.bar(
        x=c_counts.values, y=c_counts.index, orientation='h',
        color=c_counts.values, color_continuous_scale="Teal"
    )
    _ = fig_dc.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Samples", yaxis_title="",
        yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed")
    )
    show_plotly(fig_dc, filename="cancer_types")
```

## Row {height="25%"}

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Feature Missingness"
if eval_df is not None and "pct_missing" in eval_df.columns:
    miss_df = eval_df[eval_df["pct_missing"] > 0].sort_values("pct_missing", ascending=False).head(20)
    if len(miss_df) > 0:
        fig_miss = px.bar(
            miss_df, x="pct_missing", y="feature_column", orientation="h",
            color="pct_missing", color_continuous_scale="Reds"
        )
        _ = fig_miss.update_layout(
            **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
            margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
            xaxis_title="% Missing", yaxis_title=""
        )
        show_plotly(fig_miss, filename="missingness")
    else:
        display(Markdown("> No features have missing values."))
else:
    display(Markdown("> Missingness data not available. Re-run pipeline to generate."))
```

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Panel / Assay Distribution"
if "GENE_PANEL" in df.columns:
    panel_counts = df["GENE_PANEL"].value_counts()
    fig_panel = px.bar(
        x=panel_counts.values, y=panel_counts.index, orientation="h",
        color=panel_counts.values, color_continuous_scale="Purples"
    )
    _ = fig_panel.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Samples", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_panel)
else:
    display(Markdown("> Panel information not available."))
```

## Row {height="25%"}

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Per-Sample Feature Coverage"
# Histogram of non-null feature percentage per sample
if len(numeric_cols) > 0:
    completeness = df[numeric_cols].notna().mean(axis=1) * 100
    fig_cov = px.histogram(
        completeness, nbins=30,
        labels={"value": "% Features Non-Null", "count": "Samples"},
        color_discrete_sequence=[THEME["models"][0]]
    )
    _ = fig_cov.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="% Features Non-Null", yaxis_title="Samples"
    )
    show_plotly(fig_cov, filename="coverage_completeness")
```

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Per-Chromosome Features (if available)"
# Heatmap of per-chromosome features if they exist
chr_cols = [c for c in numeric_cols if c.startswith("chr") or "_chr" in c]
if len(chr_cols) > 0:
    # Show mean value per chromosome feature across all samples
    chr_means = df[chr_cols].mean()
    fig_chr = px.bar(
        x=chr_means.values, y=chr_means.index, orientation="h",
        color=chr_means.values, color_continuous_scale="Viridis",
        labels={"x": "Mean Value", "y": "Chromosome Feature"}
    )
    _ = fig_chr.update_layout(
        **LAYOUT_DEFAULTS, showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
        xaxis_title="Mean Value", yaxis_title=""
    )
    show_plotly(fig_chr, filename="chromosome_features")
else:
    display(Markdown("> No per-chromosome features detected in this feature set."))
```

# Data Explorer {scrolling="true"}

## Row

```{python}
#| title: "Feature Matrix (Grouped by Family)"
#| echo: false
from collections import defaultdict

try:
    from great_tables import GT, style, loc
    from great_tables import _tbl_data as _gt  # noqa: F401 — verify import

    # Subsample for display performance (great_tables renders full HTML)
    display_df = df.head(200).copy()

    # Auto-detect feature families from column prefixes
    feature_cols = [c for c in display_df.columns if c not in meta_cols]
    families = defaultdict(list)
    for c in feature_cols:
        parts = c.split("_")
        family = "_".join(parts[:2]) if len(parts) >= 2 else parts[0]
        families[family].append(c)

    # Build great_tables with spanners
    gt_table = GT(display_df)

    for family, cols in sorted(families.items()):
        if len(cols) > 1:
            gt_table = gt_table.tab_spanner(
                label=family.replace("_", " ").title(),
                columns=cols
            )

    # Dark theme styling to match dashboard
    gt_table = (
        gt_table
        .tab_header(
            title=f"{feature_name} Feature Matrix",
            subtitle=f"Showing first {len(display_df)} of {len(df)} samples · {len(families)} feature families"
        )
        .tab_options(
            table_background_color="#121418",
            heading_background_color="#1a1d23",
            column_labels_background_color="#1a1d23",
            table_font_color="#e0e6ed",
            heading_title_font_color="#ff9b42",
            heading_subtitle_font_color="#8b949e",
            column_labels_font_color="#aeb8c4",
            table_body_border_bottom_color="#2e3440",
            heading_border_bottom_color="rgba(255, 155, 66, 0.3)",
            column_labels_border_bottom_color="rgba(255, 155, 66, 0.2)",
            table_font_size="0.8rem",
        )
    )

    display(HTML(gt_table.as_raw_html()))

except Exception as e:
    # Fallback to itables if great_tables fails
    display(Markdown(f"> great_tables rendering failed ({e}), falling back to itables."))
    itables.show(
        df, classes="display nowrap compact",
        scrollX=True, scrollY="800px",
        paging=True, pageLength=50,
        buttons=["copy", "csv", "excel"],
        dom="Bfrtip", maxBytes=0
    )
```

```{python}
#| title: "Full Searchable Table (All Rows)"
#| echo: false
itables.show(
    df, 
    classes="display nowrap compact",
    scrollX=True, 
    scrollY="600px",
    paging=True,
    pageLength=50,
    buttons=["copy", "csv", "excel"],
    dom="Bfrtip",
    maxBytes=0
)
```
